# Phase 4 — Codegen-emitted registry & top-level re-exports

**Goal:** Automate the impl-side plumbing so every CLI generated by `ph-clint-cli` inherits the typed surface for free. Three pieces:

1. **Generate `src/framework.ts`** in every generated CLI. It imports the reactor package's document-model modules, calls `defineRegistry([...] as const)`, calls `createTypes({ configSchema, registry })`, and exports `defineCommand`/`defineTrigger`/`defineService`/`createDocumentChangeTrigger`.
2. **Fix module codegen to preserve literal `id` types.** Generated document-model modules must use `as const satisfies DocumentModelModule<…>` instead of the current `: DocumentModelModule<…>` annotation. This is what makes `defineRegistry([...] as const)` yield a registry keyed on literal documentType strings instead of collapsing to `string`.
3. **Top-level re-exports from the reactor package.** Every generated reactor package (`{impl-name}-app`) gets a root `index.ts` that re-exports document types, action creators, module objects, and guards. Impl code imports `from '{impl-name}-app'`, never from `{impl-name}-app/document-models/<name>/v1/gen/...`.

This phase is the "every new impl starts with zero friction" capstone.

## Context for future Claude

Read these first:
- [`specs/issues/ph-clint-type-safety.md`](../issues/ph-clint-type-safety.md) — problem statement. Re-read expected outcome #6.
- [`specs/stricter-typing/README.md`](./README.md) — overall plan, design decisions.
- [`specs/stricter-typing/phase-1-framework-types.md`](./phase-1-framework-types.md), [phase 2](./phase-2-createtypes-binding.md), [phase 3](./phase-3-document-change-trigger.md) — prior phases.
- [`specs/cli-planning/phase-5.md`](../cli-planning/phase-5.md) — ph-clint-cli codegen phase 5 (the broader codegen workstream; Phase 4 of stricter-typing is a subset/evolution of phase 5's emit step).
- [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — repo conventions.
- [`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/`](../../packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/) — the existing codegen internals.

**Prerequisites:** Phases 1–3 merged. You have:
- `defineRegistry`, `createTypes`, `createDocumentChangeTrigger` exported from `ph-clint`.
- `ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/framework.ts` hand-written and working.
- `ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/triggers/spec-change.ts` using the typed helper; zero casts.
- `examples/06-connect-agent/agent-cli/` migrated to use `createDocumentChangeTrigger`.

## Background — what automation buys us

Today the impl author writes, by hand:
- `src/framework.ts` (binding).
- Deep imports from `{impl-app}/document-models/<name>/v1` for document types, guards, action creators.
- Fresh casts every time a new document type is added to the project.

With Phase 4:
- `ph-clint-cli init` / `ph-clint-cli regen` emits `src/framework.ts` based on the spec's declared document types.
- Every add/remove of a document type on the `ph-clint-project` document regenerates the registry entry automatically (via the existing `spec-change` trigger from phase 4 of `cli-planning`).
- Impl code never touches deep paths.

## Scope

### Files touched

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/templates/framework.ts.tpl` | **New template.** Emits `src/framework.ts` with `defineRegistry(...)` + `createTypes(...)` + destructured exports. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/index.ts` | Add `framework.ts` to the emitted file set. Scope its writes to `mode: 'init'` AND `mode: 'update'` (overwrite safe — it's fully machine-owned). |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/spec/types.ts` | Extend `ClintProjectSpec` with `documentTypes: string[]` (if not already there) — the codegen needs to know which modules to import. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-app/document-models/ph-clint-project/v1/gen/document-model.ts` | **Via MCP** (mandatory per the ph-clint-app CLAUDE.md) — add `documentTypes: [String!]!` field to the ph-clint-project state schema. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/spec/from-document.ts` | Surface the new `documentTypes` field in `specFromDocumentState`. |
| **Upstream** — reactor package's module generator | Change `export const Foo: DocumentModelModule<FooPHState> = { … }` to `export const Foo = { … } as const satisfies DocumentModelModule<FooPHState>`. |
| **Upstream (impl-app)** — root `index.ts` generator | Generate a top-level `index.ts` that re-exports document types, module objects, type guards, action creators from each `document-models/<name>/index.ts`. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/triggers/spec-change.ts` | Update to import from `../framework.js` (generated now, not hand-written). No functional change. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/cli.ts` | Import `configSchema` from `./framework.js`. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/tests/codegen/framework.test.ts` | **New.** Unit test that codegen produces a valid `framework.ts` given a spec with N document types. |
| `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/tests/integration/regen.integration.test.ts` | Extend to cover the framework-regen case. |

### Files NOT touched

- `packages/ph-clint/src/*` — library side is stable after phase 3.
- Existing examples 01–05, 07, 08 — they don't use ph-clint-cli codegen.
- Example 06 — manually migrated in phase 3; codegen doesn't own its tree.

## Detailed design

### 1. Emit `src/framework.ts` via codegen

**Template** — `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/templates/framework.ts.tpl` (Handlebars, mirrors other templates):

```handlebars
// THIS FILE IS GENERATED BY ph-clint-cli.
// Regenerated on every `ph-clint regen` and on every spec-change tick.
// Hand edits will be overwritten — put custom bindings in cli.ts instead.

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createTypes, defineRegistry } from 'ph-clint';
{{#each documentTypes}}
import { {{moduleExportName this}} } from '{{../appPackageName}}';
{{/each}}

/**
 * The CLI's configSchema. Extend this in place — the schema itself is NOT
 * regenerated after init (the code generator only touches this file's
 * imports and the registry block). Fields declared here appear as typed
 * `ctx.context.config.<field>` in every command/trigger/service callback.
 */
export const configSchema = z.object({
  // Hand-editable region START — codegen preserves edits inside this block.
  projectDocumentId: z.string().optional(),
  // Hand-editable region END.
});

export const secretsSchema = z.object({});

/**
 * Typed document registry derived from the reactor package's document models.
 * Regenerated whenever the spec's documentTypes list changes.
 */
export const registry = defineRegistry([
{{#each documentTypes}}
  {{moduleExportName this}},
{{/each}}
] as const);

export const {
  defineCommand,
  defineTrigger,
  defineService,
  createDocumentChangeTrigger,
} = createTypes({ configSchema, registry });

export type Config = z.infer<typeof configSchema>;
export type Registry = typeof registry;
```

**Notes on the template:**

- `{{moduleExportName 'powerhouse/ph-clint-project'}}` → `PhClintProject` (PascalCase from slug). Use the existing naming helper from the codegen — do not reinvent.
- `{{../appPackageName}}` → the reactor package name (e.g. `ph-clint-app` or `{impl-name}-app`).
- The "hand-editable region" markers are a convention you'll need in the diff/patch logic: when `mode === 'update'`, read the existing `framework.ts`, extract the region between the markers, and re-emit the new file with that region preserved. If markers aren't found (e.g. user deleted them), emit a warning and overwrite. This matches how other templates handle user edits.

**If preserving hand-edits in a generated file feels fragile**, an alternative is to split:
- `src/framework.gen.ts` — fully machine-owned, regenerated every time. Contains `registry`, `createTypes(...)`, the destructured exports.
- `src/framework.ts` — hand-owned. Contains `configSchema` and re-exports what users care about from `framework.gen.ts`.

That's cleaner. Recommended. Template becomes two files:

**`framework.gen.ts.tpl`** (machine-owned):

```handlebars
// THIS FILE IS GENERATED BY ph-clint-cli. DO NOT EDIT.
import { createTypes, defineRegistry } from 'ph-clint';
{{#each documentTypes}}
import { {{moduleExportName this}} } from '{{../appPackageName}}';
{{/each}}
import { configSchema, secretsSchema } from './framework.js';

export const registry = defineRegistry([
{{#each documentTypes}}
  {{moduleExportName this}},
{{/each}}
] as const);

export const {
  defineCommand,
  defineTrigger,
  defineService,
  createDocumentChangeTrigger,
} = createTypes({ configSchema, registry });

export type Registry = typeof registry;
```

**`framework.ts.tpl`** (hand-owned, emitted on `init` only, skipped on `update`):

```handlebars
import { z } from 'zod';

export const configSchema = z.object({
  projectDocumentId: z.string().optional(),
  // Add your config fields here — this file is never overwritten after init.
});

export const secretsSchema = z.object({});

export type Config = z.infer<typeof configSchema>;

export {
  registry,
  defineCommand,
  defineTrigger,
  defineService,
  createDocumentChangeTrigger,
  type Registry,
} from './framework.gen.js';
```

Go with the split. It's what the rest of the codegen uses for the docs/actions split.

### 2. Extend the `ClintProjectSpec`

`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/spec/types.ts`:

```ts
export const clintProjectSpecSchema = z.object({
  // … existing fields …
  documentTypes: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
});
```

`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/spec/from-document.ts`:

```ts
export function specFromDocumentState(state: PhClintProjectGlobalState): ClintProjectSpec | null {
  // … existing name-required-check …
  const parsed = clintProjectSpecSchema.safeParse({
    name: state.name,
    // … existing fields …
    documentTypes: state.documentTypes ?? [],
  });
  return parsed.success ? parsed.data : null;
}
```

The underlying document model's `documentTypes` field needs to exist. Follow the mandatory ph-clint-app workflow (read `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-app/CLAUDE.md`):

1. Use MCP tool `mcp__reactor-mcp__getDocumentModelSchema` with `type: "powerhouse/document-model"` first.
2. Dispatch `SET_STATE_SCHEMA` on the ph-clint-project document to add `documentTypes: [String!]!` (with default `[]`).
3. Update `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-app/document-models/ph-clint-project/src/reducers/*.ts` manually to handle any new operation if needed (for now `documentTypes` would just be part of the state — no new operation needed unless you also want an `ADD_DOCUMENT_TYPE` action).
4. Run `npm run tsc` and `npm run lint:fix` in `ph-clint-app/` to validate.

If you're punting on adding a state field in this phase (reasonable — it's a spec surface bump), alternative: derive `documentTypes` from the spec's existing `features.powerhouse` block by scanning the target `app/document-models/` folder at codegen time. This keeps the spec stable but couples the generator to filesystem scanning. Either is fine; prefer the state-field path for explicitness.

### 3. Fix `satisfies` in reactor-package module generator

This is **upstream of ph-clint** — the reactor package is generated by Powerhouse's codegen (`@powerhousedao/...`), not by ph-clint. Two options:

**Option A (preferred): push a PR upstream.** Change the generated module template to emit:

```ts
export const PhClintProject = {
  version: 1,
  reducer,
  actions,
  utils,
  documentModel: createState(defaultBaseState(), documentModel),
} as const satisfies DocumentModelModule<PhClintProjectPHState>;
```

instead of:

```ts
export const PhClintProject: DocumentModelModule<PhClintProjectPHState> = {
  // …
};
```

The `as const satisfies` form preserves `documentModel.id`'s literal type while still type-checking the module's shape. This fix is free for all Powerhouse consumers, not just ph-clint.

**Option B (fallback): post-process in ph-clint-cli codegen.** When ph-clint-cli regenerates the impl-app side, it runs a text transform on `document-models/*/v*/module.ts` to swap the annotation for `satisfies`. Fragile, but it works if Powerhouse doesn't accept the PR. Document this as a known workaround in ph-clint-cli's `README.md` and add a regression test.

**Option C (worst — don't do this):** Define a local ph-clint-specific helper `asConstModule(mod)` that re-types the module at the import site. Forces users to wrap every import; defeats the point.

Start with **Option A** — it's one PR. Keep **Option B** ready if the PR is rejected or blocked.

**Acceptance test for this fix**: in the `.test-d.ts`, assert:

```ts
expectTypeOf<
  (typeof PhClintProject)['documentModel']['id']
>().toEqualTypeOf<'powerhouse/ph-clint-project'>();
```

If this passes with the upstream fix merged, the whole literal-inference chain works.

### 4. Top-level re-exports from the reactor package

The impl's reactor package (`ph-clint-app` in our case; `{impl-name}-app` in user projects) needs a root `index.ts` that re-exports everything impls actually use. Target state:

```ts
// {impl-name}-app/index.ts — generated
// Document types
export type * from './document-models/ph-clint-project/index.js';
// Module objects (for defineRegistry)
export { PhClintProject } from './document-models/ph-clint-project/index.js';
// If more docs are added, they get re-exported here automatically.

// Drives, editors, processors, etc., if any — added as they appear.
```

**Who owns this file?** Powerhouse's `ph` CLI generates `document-models/*/index.ts`. Ph-clint-cli's codegen generates the top-level `index.ts`. Template:

**`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/codegen/templates/app-index.ts.tpl`**:

```handlebars
{{#each documentTypes}}
export * from './document-models/{{slugify this}}/index.js';
{{/each}}
```

(Assuming `./document-models/<slug>/index.ts` already re-exports the module, document types, guards, and action creators. The existing generated `index.ts` we saw in ph-clint-app does exactly that: `export * from "./v1/index.js"; export * from "./upgrades/index.js";`. Chain up one level.)

**Emit on every regen**, both `init` and `update`. Fully machine-owned; no hand-editable region needed.

### 5. Update ph-clint-cli's own source to dogfood

Now that the codegen exists, ph-clint-cli itself stops being a special case:

- `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/framework.ts` — was hand-written in Phase 2/3. Regenerate it. Delete it from source and let the codegen emit it on the next `ph-clint regen` (run it manually once during this phase to bootstrap).
- `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/cli.ts` — imports `configSchema` from `./framework.js`, which now re-exports from `./framework.gen.js`.
- `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/triggers/spec-change.ts` — no change. Already imports from `./framework.js`.

Run a regen cycle manually during Phase 4 impl and commit the generated files. Verify they match the hand-written version semantically.

### 6. Add the ph-clint-app top-level `index.ts`

Run ph-clint-cli's codegen against the ph-clint-app directory, or hand-write it once and let future regens maintain it:

```ts
// packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-app/index.ts
export * from './document-models/ph-clint-project/index.js';
```

Then update any remaining deep imports to use the top-level form:

```bash
# audit
grep -rn "from 'ph-clint-app/document-models" packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/
# should be zero after phase 4
```

## Tests

### Runtime tests

**`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/tests/codegen/framework.test.ts`**

Given a fake spec with documentTypes `['powerhouse/a', 'powerhouse/b']`, assert that `generateProject` emits:

- `src/framework.gen.ts` with:
  - `import { A } from '<appPackageName>';`
  - `import { B } from '<appPackageName>';`
  - `defineRegistry([A, B] as const)`
  - Destructured `createTypes` exports.
- `src/framework.ts` on `mode: 'init'` only. On `mode: 'update'`, `framework.ts` is NOT overwritten (user may have edited `configSchema`).
- `{app}/index.ts` re-exports `./document-models/a/index.js` and `./document-models/b/index.js`.

**`packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/tests/integration/regen.integration.test.ts` (extend)**

Existing test covers the regen flow. Add assertions:

1. After `ph-clint init` on a fresh directory, `src/framework.ts` and `src/framework.gen.ts` both exist.
2. After editing `src/framework.ts` to add a config field, running `ph-clint regen` preserves the edit.
3. After editing `src/framework.gen.ts` by hand, running `ph-clint regen` overwrites the edit (user was warned; test that the file returns to its canonical form).
4. Adding a new documentType to the spec (via direct document state manipulation or mock) and triggering regen adds the corresponding import + registry entry to `framework.gen.ts`.

### Compile-time tests

Add to `packages/ph-clint/tests/types/`:

**`literal-inference.test-d.ts`** — must run **after** the upstream `satisfies` fix lands:

```ts
import { expectTypeOf } from 'expect-type';
import { PhClintProject } from 'ph-clint-app';  // via top-level re-export
import { defineRegistry } from 'ph-clint';

// Literal preserved?
expectTypeOf<(typeof PhClintProject)['documentModel']['id']>()
  .toEqualTypeOf<'powerhouse/ph-clint-project'>();

// Registry keys are literal, not `string`.
const registry = defineRegistry([PhClintProject] as const);
type Keys = keyof typeof registry;
expectTypeOf<Keys>().toEqualTypeOf<'powerhouse/ph-clint-project'>();
```

If this test compiles, the whole Phase 4 typing chain works end-to-end.

### Regression

- `pnpm test && pnpm build` in `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/`.
- `ph-clint regen` in the ph-clint-cli working directory produces a clean diff (no unexpected file changes on first regen after checkout).
- `pnpm test && pnpm build` in every example.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ph-clint init` (on a fresh dir) emits `src/framework.ts` and `src/framework.gen.ts`, and a root `index.ts` in the reactor package.
- [ ] `ph-clint regen` overwrites `framework.gen.ts` and preserves `framework.ts`.
- [ ] `spec-change` trigger's regeneration path also refreshes `framework.gen.ts` when the spec's `documentTypes` list changes.
- [ ] Upstream reactor-package module codegen emits `as const satisfies DocumentModelModule<…>` (PR merged OR post-process fallback in place and tested).
- [ ] `(typeof PhClintProject)['documentModel']['id']` is `'powerhouse/ph-clint-project'` (literal), not `string`.
- [ ] `defineRegistry([PhClintProject] as const)` produces a registry keyed on the literal documentType string.
- [ ] `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/` contains **zero** deep imports of `ph-clint-app/document-models/<name>/…`. All such imports go through the top-level `ph-clint-app` re-export.
- [ ] Generated sample project (via `ph-clint init` in a temp dir) builds and runs with `pnpm build` + `pnpm start` — end-to-end smoke test.
- [ ] Coverage for `packages/ph-clint-cli/` stays within tolerance (it's not held to 95% like ph-clint proper, but shouldn't regress significantly).

## Pitfalls & decisions

1. **Split `framework.ts` / `framework.gen.ts`**: Recommended strongly. The alternative (single file with hand-editable regions) is a well-known source of diff churn and user frustration. The split is one extra file; it removes all ambiguity about what's user-owned vs machine-owned.

2. **Upstream PR vs fork**: The `satisfies` change is genuinely better for Powerhouse's own users (literal types downstream of their generator). It's a one-line template change. Invest in the PR; keep Option B as a fallback only.

3. **What about existing generated modules in impls that predate Phase 4?** None exist yet — no production impls. When the upstream fix lands, consumers regenerate and pick it up automatically. If the PR takes a while and we ship Phase 4 with the text-transform fallback, document the fallback's visibility in ph-clint-cli's README so users know why their regen diff includes module-file tweaks.

4. **Don't regenerate `framework.ts` on `update`**: It holds `configSchema`, which is the single most user-edited file in an impl. Overwriting it is hostile. Only emit on `init`.

5. **Don't put `configSchema` in `framework.gen.ts`**: Same reason. `configSchema` is the one piece of this wiring that is genuinely user-owned.

6. **Top-level `{impl-app}/index.ts` is a new file that may conflict with Powerhouse's own emit**: Verify. If Powerhouse already generates a top-level index, either augment it (via append markers) or convince Powerhouse to include our re-exports. Likely Powerhouse's codegen doesn't emit a top-level barrel — check and confirm.

7. **`documentTypes` field on the spec**: Adding a new state field to a Powerhouse document model is non-trivial — it requires a codegen cycle on `ph-clint-app` itself (which is a reactor package). Coordinate this with the ph-clint-cli planning `specs/cli-planning/phase-5.md` work if that phase hasn't landed; otherwise this phase's scope becomes "change the spec document model" plus "regenerate" plus "update the converter" plus "update the codegen". Budget accordingly.

8. **What if an impl wants registry entries NOT from its reactor package?** (E.g., to also watch documents from a remote reactor it doesn't own the types for.) Fine — they can hand-write `defineRegistry([...])` in `framework.ts` and skip `framework.gen.ts`. Codegen emits the `.gen.ts` form as the default; users can opt out by deleting it and inlining the registry into `framework.ts`. Document this as an escape hatch.

9. **Naming collision risk in re-exports**: If two document models define a type called `State`, top-level `export *` collides. Prevention: generated `document-models/<name>/index.ts` should use named exports scoped by document model name (e.g., `PhClintProjectState`, not `State`). Most generated code already does this — verify.

10. **CI / smoke test**: Because this phase touches codegen, add a CI step (or a manual script) that runs `ph-clint init` in a temp directory, installs deps, builds the result, and asserts the build passes. Protects against template regressions.

## Rollout checklist

1. Upstream PR to Powerhouse: change reactor-package module generator to emit `as const satisfies DocumentModelModule<…>`.
2. While waiting for merge: implement Option B fallback in ph-clint-cli codegen. Add regression test.
3. Add `documentTypes: [String!]!` to `ph-clint-project` document model via the mandatory MCP workflow. Update reducers and `from-document.ts`. Run `npm run tsc && npm run lint:fix` in `ph-clint-app/`.
4. Extend `clintProjectSpecSchema` with `documentTypes: z.array(z.string()).default([])`.
5. Write `framework.gen.ts.tpl` and `framework.ts.tpl` templates.
6. Write `app-index.ts.tpl` for the top-level reactor-package barrel.
7. Wire the new templates into `codegen/index.ts`. Respect `mode: 'init' | 'update'` semantics: `framework.gen.ts` and `app-index.ts` regen every time; `framework.ts` only on init.
8. Write `tests/codegen/framework.test.ts` unit tests.
9. Extend `tests/integration/regen.integration.test.ts` for the four cases above.
10. Run `ph-clint regen` in `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/`. Commit the regenerated `framework.gen.ts` and reactor-package `index.ts`.
11. Delete the hand-written `framework.ts` file if codegen's emitted version matches; otherwise reconcile by hand. (Phase 2/3 wrote it manually; codegen output should be identical modulo whitespace.)
12. Grep for any remaining `ph-clint-app/document-models/` deep imports in `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/src/`. Replace with top-level re-export imports.
13. `pnpm test && pnpm build` in `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-cli/` and `packages/ph-clint-cli/ph-clint-app/`.
14. `pnpm build` in every example.
15. Run an end-to-end smoke: `cd $(mktemp -d) && node <path-to-ph-clint-cli> init --name smoke-test && cd smoke-test-cli && pnpm install && pnpm build`.
16. Commit: `feat(codegen): emit framework.ts registry binding + top-level re-exports (phase 4)`.
